description:
You are working as a penetration tester and must prepare an isolated Python 3
    workspace before you start running vulnerability-scanning utilities.  
    Set up a virtual environment named “vulnscan_env” directly under your home
    directory using Python 3’s built-in “venv” module.

    Requirements the automated checker will verify after you are finished:
        1. The directory “/home/user/vulnscan_env” exists.
        2. Inside that directory a file named “pyvenv.cfg” exists.
        3. The file “/home/user/vulnscan_env/bin/python” exists and is an
           executable ELF binary (i.e., the venv’s interpreter).

    Constraints:
        • Accomplish the setup with a single terminal command that invokes the
          venv module.
        • No activation of the environment, no package installation and no
          additional output files are required.
        • Do not create the directory manually—let the venv command create it.

    When the command completes successfully you may see no console output; that
    is fine.  The grader cares only about the filesystem artifacts listed above.
truth:
The grading script will validate the following ground-truth conditions:

    1. os.path.isdir("/home/user/vulnscan_env") is True
    2. os.path.isfile("/home/user/vulnscan_env/pyvenv.cfg") is True
    3. os.path.isfile("/home/user/vulnscan_env/bin/python") is True
       and os.access("/home/user/vulnscan_env/bin/python", os.X_OK) is True
    4. The “/home/user/vulnscan_env/bin/python” ELF header begins with
       bytes 0x7f, 0x45, 0x4c, 0x46 (“\x7fELF”).
name: task_000000_37f07946
